metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the env var (e.g., `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR=<persistent-worktree-path>/.tickets-tracker`) set in the Dockerfile entrypoint / `docker-compose.yml` that wires `config.tracker_dir` to the persistent tickets worktree, so `rebar.create_ticket` / `rebar.link` in the voter emit path resolve to the correct store without a `repo_root` argument.
findings.finding: The emit path (create_ticket + link) runs OUTSIDE the `with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory as repo_root:` block (after `store.record_vote`, which is already outside it in voter.py), so `repo_root` is None or a deleted path at emit time. `rebar.create_ticket` resolves the tracker via `config.tracker_dir(repo_root=None)`, which falls back to `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` env var or `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` of cwd (`/app`). The plan's infra section specifies a persistent tickets worktree clone but never specifies setting `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR` (or equivalent) to point at it. Without this wiring, `create_ticket` will resolve to the wrong dir or fail entirely, silently (the emit is best-effort).
findings.location: Section C ('Reviewbot emit + trailer linking') and Section D ('Container: infra/compose/Dockerfile.reviewbot') — the connection between the persistent worktree and `config.tracker_dir` resolution
findings.suggested_fix: In Section D, add: 'Set `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR=/var/gerrit/site/reviewbot/tickets/.tickets-tracker` in the reviewbot container environment (in `docker-compose.yml`'s `environment:` block or as an `ENV` in the Dockerfile) AFTER the worktree is materialized, so `config.tracker_dir(None)` resolves to it from anywhere in the process (including the post-vote emit path which runs outside the temp-dir `with` block).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Anchor the AC4 Dockerfile grep to the actual config key: replace `grep -q 'credential'` with `grep -q 'credential\.'` or more precisely `grep -qE 'credential\.[^=]+\.helper'` to distinguish the functional config line from comments.
findings.finding: The AC4 proving command `grep -q 'credential' infra/compose/Dockerfile.reviewbot` is a substring false-positive: the word 'credential' could appear in comments, unrelated ENV vars, or documentation lines in the Dockerfile, satisfying the grep without the URL-scoped credential helper actually being wired. This is verify-command defect taxonomy item 1 (substring false-positive).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 proving command: `grep -q 'credential' infra/compose/Dockerfile.reviewbot`
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the Dockerfile grep in AC4 with: `grep -qE 'git config.*credential\.[^.]+\.helper' infra/compose/Dockerfile.reviewbot`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify and implement the mechanism for voter.py to locate the persistent tickets worktree when calling rebar.create_ticket/list_tickets/link: either set REBAR_TRACKER_DIR in docker-compose.yml env to the mounted worktree path, or pass an explicit `repo_root=<worktree_path>` to every rebar store call in the emit block — and document which approach is chosen.
findings.finding: The voter.py artifact-emit code (rebar.create_ticket / rebar.list_tickets / rebar.link calls) must resolve the tickets store, but the plan does not specify the mechanism — no repo_root argument, no REBAR_TRACKER_DIR env var, and the reviewbot container's working directory (/app) has no .tickets-tracker. config.tracker_dir(None) will resolve against /app (no .tickets-tracker there), so the emit calls will silently fail on the best-effort path, producing zero artifacts despite no error surface to the operator.
findings.location: Section C (Reviewbot emit), 'Implementation precision' final paragraph, and infra/compose/docker-compose.yml / Dockerfile.reviewbot
findings.suggested_fix: Add `REBAR_TRACKER_DIR=/var/rebar/tickets-worktree` to the review-bot service's environment in docker-compose.yml (matching the bind-mounted persistent worktree volume path), and verify rebar.create_ticket/list_tickets/link inherit it; OR thread an explicit `repo_root=cfg.tickets_worktree_path` through every store call in the voter emit block (requires a new ReceiverConfig field).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact Dockerfile change needed to run credential-helper initialization before uvicorn: either introduce a shell-script ENTRYPOINT that runs `git config` + worktree clone then `exec uvicorn ...`, or add an inline RUN step with a static helper script — and confirm the URL scope key format matches the tickets_remote value.
findings.finding: The Dockerfile.reviewbot currently has a CMD directive but no ENTRYPOINT. The plan's 'Implementation precision' states the URL-scoped git credential helper is 'set by the Dockerfile entrypoint before the worktree clone', but there is no ENTRYPOINT in the existing Dockerfile — adding credential-helper initialization requires either converting CMD to an ENTRYPOINT shell script or adding a separate setup step. The plan does not name the specific git config key format (e.g. `git config --global credential.https://github.com/.helper '...'`) needed to scope it to the tickets remote URL only.
findings.location: Section D, 'Container' bullet; 'Implementation precision' final paragraph; infra/compose/Dockerfile.reviewbot

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit step in the Scope/Files section declaring that `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py`'s `emit()` and `build_payload()` signatures will be extended with `change_id`, `revision`, and `change_fp` keyword parameters, and identify all existing callers of `emit()` (at minimum `produce_code_review_verdict`) that must be updated or kept backward-compatible via defaults.
findings.finding: The plan calls `code_review.sidecar.emit(verdict, target_ticket=…, change_id=…, revision=…, change_fp=…)` in Section C, but the ACTUAL `emit()` signature in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` is `emit(verdict, *, target_ticket, repo_root=None)` — no `change_id`, `revision`, or `change_fp` parameters exist. The plan does not call out that these parameters must be added to the existing `emit` function (which is already called by `produce_code_review_verdict`), making it an undeclared signature change with existing-caller impact.
findings.location: Section C — `code_review.sidecar.emit(verdict, target_ticket=<artifact id>, change_id=…, revision=…, change_fp=…)`
findings.suggested_fix: Add `change_id: str = ''`, `revision: str = ''`, `change_fp: str = ''` as keyword-only args with defaults so the existing WS4 call path (which omits them) is unaffected. Make this explicit in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the required test functions (or at minimum the required assertion categories) within `tests/unit/test_code_review_artifact.py` in AC1 — e.g. 'must include assertions for: type in schema, composer._TYPES, _NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES exclusion, gate-exempt pass, EXCLUDED_SYNC_TYPES, default-list hidden / search visible' — so the proving command cannot pass on a partial implementation.
findings.finding: AC1's single proving command (`pytest tests/unit/test_code_review_artifact.py -v`) must cover all guard-site exemptions — schema, composer._TYPES, reducer predicate broadening, all graph callers, gates, plan-review short-circuit, Jira exclusion, export exclusion, and reads list-visibility — but the plan never specifies what assertions the test file must contain, leaving the test free to cover only a subset while the proving command still passes green.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 proving command; Section A (guard sites list)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the contradiction in Section C: either add `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py` to the scope/files list (its return dict is extended with a `verdict` key) or describe explicitly how the voter obtains the raw four-pass verdict without re-invoking produce_code_review_verdict and without an adapter.py change.
findings.finding: adapter.code_review_decision currently returns {decision, message, findings, coverage_gap} — it does NOT expose the raw four-pass verdict dict. The plan says 'thread the full verdict up from adapter.code_review_decision (a new verdict key on its return)' but also says 'NO caller change (the voter still reads decision + message)'. These are contradictory: if a verdict key is added to the return, the voter IS changed to read it; if the voter is unchanged, the verdict is not available. The adapter.py return-shape extension is not listed in scope/files.
findings.location: Section C (Reviewbot emit + trailer linking) / src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py` to the Scope/files list and clarify: 'code_review_decision returns {decision, message, findings, coverage_gap, verdict} — the raw produce_code_review_verdict dict — so the voter can pass verdict to the sidecar without a second LLM call.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the updated signatures for `code_review.sidecar.emit()` and `build_payload()` — including the new `change_id`, `revision`, and `change_fp` keyword parameters — and state explicitly how per-finding `norm_id` is added to the payload (e.g., iterate `blocking`+`advisory` lists and inject `norm_id(f)` into each finding dict before serializing).
findings.finding: The plan's call to `code_review.sidecar.emit(verdict, target_ticket=<id>, change_id=…, revision=…, change_fp=…)` is incompatible with the existing `emit()` signature `(verdict, *, target_ticket, repo_root=None)` — the `change_id`, `revision`, and `change_fp` keyword arguments are not accepted, and `build_payload()` also does not accept them.
findings.location: Section C ('Emit the verdict sidecar … by calling code_review.sidecar.emit(verdict, target_ticket=<artifact id>, change_id=…, revision=…, change_fp=…)'); src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py lines 22-55
findings.suggested_fix: Update the plan to specify: `build_payload(verdict, *, target_ticket, change_id='', revision='', change_fp='')` adding `change_id`, `revision`, `change_fingerprint` top-level keys to the returned dict, and iterating `blocking`+`advisory` to inject `norm_id` on each finding. Update `emit()` to accept and forward those parameters to `build_payload`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare in Section D and the SSM runbook that the `reviewbot-tickets-pat` MUST be a GitHub fine-grained PAT scoped to the single `navapbc/rebar` repository with `Contents: Read and write` permission only (not a classic PAT), and add this requirement to the operator deploy-prerequisite note alongside the SSM slot definition in `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`.
findings.finding: The plan specifies a 'RebarBotNava contents:write PAT' for the git push to GitHub but does not declare whether this is a fine-grained, repository-scoped PAT (minimum: write to refs/heads/tickets on navapbc/rebar only) or a classic OAuth PAT (account-wide write access to all repos). A classic PAT would grant far broader write access than the single-branch push requires, violating least-privilege on the new boundary-crossing credential path. The access posture is undeclared.
findings.location: Section D (Infra) — SSM slot 'reviewbot-tickets-pat'; Implementation precision section — 'git credential helper'; AC bullet 4 ('reviewbot-tickets-pat' SSM slot)
findings.suggested_fix: In `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`, update the inline comment for `reviewbot-tickets-pat` to read: 'A GitHub **fine-grained** PAT for the RebarBotNava account, scoped to the navapbc/rebar repository with Contents: Read and write permission ONLY (not a classic PAT). Do not use a classic PAT — scope must be repo-specific to satisfy least-privilege.' Mirror this note in the Section D deploy-prerequisite description.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify the update path for the idempotency hit case: define whether the sidecar is overwritten (preferred for freshness) or skipped, and document this in Section C.
findings.finding: The idempotency path says it 'reuses/updates' an existing artifact when the same (change-id, revision) is reviewed again, but the update mechanism for the sidecar is not specified: does it overwrite the existing sidecar entry, append a new one, or skip emit entirely? This leaves the shared-state update semantics undefined.
findings.location: Section C (Reviewbot emit), 'Idempotency' paragraph; Implementation precision section
findings.suggested_fix: When the lookup finds an existing artifact, call `sidecar.emit(..., target_ticket=<existing_id>, overwrite=True)` to replace the stale payload; document this as the canonical update path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly call out in Section C that `adapter.code_review_decision` must be modified to also return the raw `verdict` dict as a `"verdict"` key (a NEW addition to its previously-stable return shape), and note that the adapter's 'stable return shape' docstring must be updated to reflect this contract extension.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that the voter can thread 'the FULL verdict' from `adapter.code_review_decision` via 'a new `verdict` key on its return', but the actual adapter return dict is `{decision, message, findings, coverage_gap}` — no `verdict` key is returned today. The plan's own adapter docstring says 'Signature + return shape are stable (the voter is unchanged)'. Adding a `verdict` key is a substantive, stable-contract-breaking change that is not called out as such; if the implementation adds it without updating the stable-contract docstring, or if the implementer misreads the plan as 'this already exists', the voter emit step silently gets `None`.
findings.location: Section C ('Reviewbot emit + trailer linking') — 'the FULL verdict is threaded up from `adapter.code_review_decision` (a new `verdict` key on its return)'
findings.suggested_fix: In Section C, add a sentence: 'This requires modifying `adapter.code_review_decision` to expose the raw `produce_code_review_verdict` dict as `verdict["verdict_raw"]` (or similar) in its return dict; the adapter docstring claiming a stable return shape must be updated to document this addition.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the value of the new `verdict` key in `adapter.code_review_decision`'s return dict for ALL return paths (PASS, BLOCK-finding, and all coverage-gap/error paths via `_block()`), and define the voter's fallback rule when `verdict` is absent or `{}` (e.g., skip artifact emit, or emit a `coverage_gap=True` artifact with an empty findings list).
findings.finding: The adapter's return schema is not specified for the new `verdict` key in error/coverage-gap code paths: `_block()` returns `{decision, message, findings, coverage_gap}` with no `verdict` key, so the voter's artifact-emit path (which reads `decision['verdict']`) receives `None` on every non-PASS/error path — but the plan provides no fallback rule for this case.
findings.location: Section C ('the FULL verdict is threaded up from adapter.code_review_decision (a new `verdict` key on its return)'); src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py lines 150-218
findings.suggested_fix: In adapter.py, add `'verdict': verdict` to the PASS return dict and `'verdict': verdict` (the partial verdict dict) to `_block()`'s return; when `verdict` is `{}` (error paths), still return `'verdict': {}`. In voter.py, guard the artifact emit with `if decision.get('verdict') is not None` or always emit with the (possibly empty) verdict, documenting the choice.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the tickets-worktree as a named external volume in `docker-compose.yml` (e.g. `reviewbot_tickets_worktree: external: true`) mounted into the container, and add the corresponding `docker volume create` step to `compose-up.sh` — mirroring the `gerrit_reviewbot` pattern.
findings.finding: The persistent tickets-worktree volume is not named or declared in `docker-compose.yml`'s `volumes:` block, leaving its persistence model unspecified — the worktree may be lost on every container rebuild (auto-deploy) if it lives only in the image layer.
findings.location: Section D (Infra) — `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` and `infra/compose/Dockerfile.reviewbot` for the 'persistent tickets worktree'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `reviewbot_tickets_worktree: external: true` under `volumes:` in docker-compose.yml, mount it at e.g. `/var/rebar/tickets-worktree` in the `review-bot` service, and add `docker volume create --driver local --opt type=none --opt o=bind --opt device=/var/gerrit/site/tickets-worktree reviewbot_tickets_worktree` to compose-up.sh. The Dockerfile entrypoint then clones into that path only if not already initialized.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Treat `reviewbot-tickets-pat` as OPTIONAL in `fetch-secrets.sh` (use `get_param_optional` with a downstream fail-loud check in the reviewbot only when `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=always` is set), or add explicit sequencing documentation that the SSM slot MUST be populated before deploying this code — and verify this does not break stack startup.
findings.finding: Adding `reviewbot-tickets-pat` as a REQUIRED entry in `fetch-secrets.sh` (via `get_param`, not `get_param_optional`) makes the entire compose stack fail to start if the SSM slot is not yet populated — blocking Gerrit and the review-bot even when the new artifact-emit feature is not yet activated.
findings.location: Section D (Infra) — `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` mapping; `infra/terraform/ssm.tf` deploy-step prerequisite note
findings.suggested_fix: Use `get_param_optional github-oauth-client-id`-style handling for `reviewbot-tickets-pat` and add a startup check inside the reviewbot container: if `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=always` but `REVIEWBOT_TICKETS_PAT` is empty, log a WARNING and degrade to emit-disabled mode rather than aborting the stack.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in Section A that the `_hierarchy.py` edit must broaden both literal `== 'session_log'` blocking-link refusal checks (lines 175 and 181) to `in _NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES`, not merely add `code_review: 0` to `_TYPE_TIER`.
findings.finding: The plan states that `graph/_hierarchy.py`'s 'blocking-link refusal reads the constant directly' (the new `_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES` constant). However, the ACTUAL code in `_hierarchy.py` lines 175 and 181 uses two hardcoded literal string comparisons (`source_state.get('ticket_type') == 'session_log'` and `target_state.get('ticket_type') == 'session_log'`), not a shared constant. The plan needs to explicitly call out that these two literal-string refusals must be broadened to check the new shared set, not merely that the constant 'is read'.
findings.location: Section A — 'graph/_hierarchy.py is edited EXPLICITLY (NOT via the flag): its _TYPE_TIER gains `code_review: 0` and its blocking-link refusal reads the constant directly'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace both `state.get('ticket_type') == 'session_log'` guards in `resolve_hierarchy_link` with `state.get('ticket_type') in _NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES` and import the constant from the new shared module.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly note in Section A that _hierarchy.py has TWO separate blocking-link guard sites (source and target, lines ~175 and ~181) and both must be updated to check membership in _NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES.
findings.finding: The _hierarchy.py blocking-link guard currently has TWO separate equality checks (`source_state.get('ticket_type') == 'session_log'` and `target_state.get('ticket_type') == 'session_log'`). The plan says to edit _hierarchy.py 'EXPLICITLY (NOT via the flag): its _TYPE_TIER gains code_review: 0 and its blocking-link refusal reads the constant directly' — but two guard sites need editing, and the plan only describes the edit once. The scope description could lead an implementer to miss the target-side guard.
findings.location: Section A (Store/graph exclusion) / src/rebar/graph/_hierarchy.py lines 175 and 181
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the _hierarchy.py edit description: 'Update BOTH the source_state and target_state type guards (two separate if-blocks) to check ticket_type not in _NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the canonical module path of `_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES` (e.g., `rebar.reducer._api` or a new `rebar._artifact_types` leaf) and the exact replacement predicate for the two `== 'session_log'` checks in `_hierarchy.py:170-186` (e.g., `if source_state.get('ticket_type') in _NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES:`).
findings.finding: The plan specifies that `_hierarchy.py`'s `session_log` blocking-link refusal 'reads the constant directly' (`_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES`), but the actual code has two hardcoded string-equality checks (`== 'session_log'`) with no constant; the plan does not specify the exact replacement form, leaving an ambiguous implementation decision for the executor.
findings.location: Section A ('graph/_hierarchy.py is edited EXPLICITLY (NOT via the flag): its _TYPE_TIER gains code_review: 0 and its blocking-link refusal reads the constant directly'); src/rebar/graph/_hierarchy.py lines 170-186
findings.suggested_fix: Define `_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES = frozenset({'session_log', 'code_review'})` in `reducer/_api.py` (already imported by _hierarchy.py indirectly via _loader). Replace both `== 'session_log'` guards in _hierarchy.py with `in _NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES` and add a single import.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the home module for `_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES` to the Scope file list (e.g., `src/rebar/schemas/constants.py` or `src/rebar/reducer/_api.py` with an explicit import path for `reads.py`).
findings.finding: The plan describes `_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES` as a 'new shared module constant' consumed by both `reducer/_api.py` and `reads.py` (different sub-packages), but the Scope section lists neither a new shared constants file nor a home module for this constant. The two call sites are in different packages (`reducer/` and `_engine_support/`), so the implementer must guess the module location — risking either a circular import or an ad-hoc placement that diverges from what the plan implies.
findings.location: Section A ('a new shared module constant'); Scope section (file list)
findings.suggested_fix: Add one line to the Scope section: `src/rebar/_ticket_types.py` (or similar top-level constants module) as the home for `_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES`, then reference it explicitly in both Section A and the Implementation Precision section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific module that will own `_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES` (e.g., `src/rebar/reducer/_api.py` or a new `src/rebar/reducer/_constants.py`) and add it to the scope/files list.
findings.finding: The new shared constant `_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES` is referenced throughout the plan (Section A, Implementation precision) but is never assigned to a specific module file, and no such module or constant exists in the codebase today. The scope/files list does not include the file that would own it.
findings.location: Section A (Store/graph exclusion) / Implementation precision
findings.suggested_fix: Define _NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES in src/rebar/reducer/_api.py (already edited for the predicate change) and import it in reads.py and _hierarchy.py — add those import-line edits to the scope description.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a feature flag (e.g. `REBAR_EMIT_CODE_REVIEW_ARTIFACTS` env var, defaulting to `false` initially) to gate the voter.py emit path so the artifact-write behavior can be enabled/disabled without a container redeploy.
findings.finding: The emit behavior change (voter.py artifact write on every review) is a single 100%-traffic flip with no feature flag or gradual rollout mechanism — every review after deploy will attempt artifact writes. The only 'canary' mentioned is the live PAT push verification, not a toggle for the emit path itself.
findings.location: Section D (Infra) and Section C (Reviewbot emit); AC bullet 4
findings.suggested_fix: In voter.py, guard the entire emit block with `if os.getenv('REBAR_EMIT_CODE_REVIEW_ARTIFACTS', 'false').lower() == 'true':`. Add the env var to docker-compose.yml (default false), promote to true after the deploy canary passes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback path for the `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=always` infra change to Section D (e.g., 'to disable: set `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=never` and redeploy; the reviewbot continues to review normally, artifact emit is suppressed').
findings.finding: The plan sets `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=always` as a persistent env-var in `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` — a behavioral change to the live reviewbot container that currently writes only ephemeral `/tmp` snapshots — but provides no explicit rollback or back-out path for this infra change. The plan acknowledges failures are non-fatal and the push is a deploy-step canary, but acknowledgement alone does not satisfy T4's requirement for an explicit rollback/back-out mechanism.
findings.location: Section D ('set `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=always`'); AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-sentence back-out note to Section D: 'Back-out: set `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=never` (or remove the env var) and redeploy — the vote path is unaffected; artifact emission silently no-ops.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether include_session_logs=True should also opt-in code_review exports, or whether a new separate parameter (e.g., include_code_reviews) is needed — and update the AC and export_ndjson scope accordingly.
findings.finding: The export_ndjson.py broadening is ambiguous: the parameter is named include_session_logs on both the internal function and the public rebar.export_tickets() API. The plan says to 'broaden the hardcoded session_log guard so default NDJSON exports exclude code_review too (opt in via include_session_logs)' — but if include_session_logs=True opts in BOTH session_log AND code_review, the semantics of the existing public API change silently for callers that pass include_session_logs=True expecting only session_logs.
findings.location: Section A (Export) / src/rebar/_io/export_ndjson.py

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the required deploy order (SSM secret populated → `fetch-secrets.sh` run → container start) and add a startup assertion in the Dockerfile entrypoint that fails fast (exits non-zero) if `REVIEWBOT_TICKETS_PAT` is empty.
findings.finding: The deploy ordering between SSM secret population and container start is unspecified. If the container starts before the operator populates the `reviewbot-tickets-pat` SSM slot, `REVIEWBOT_TICKETS_PAT` will be empty/absent and the git credential helper will silently fail or push unauthenticated.
findings.location: Section D (Infra); AC bullet 4; `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh`, `infra/terraform/ssm.tf`
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Dockerfile entrypoint: `[ -n "$REVIEWBOT_TICKETS_PAT" ] || { echo 'REVIEWBOT_TICKETS_PAT is required' >&2; exit 1; }`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `_FILE_IMPACT_EXEMPT_TYPES` in `composer.py` as an explicit check to AC1 (e.g., verify `code_review in composer._FILE_IMPACT_EXEMPT_TYPES` in `test_code_review_artifact.py`).
findings.finding: Section A lists `composer.py`'s `_FILE_IMPACT_EXEMPT_TYPES` as a guard site that must include `code_review`, but AC1 does not verify this specific exemption. The AC1 list covers 'reducer/dependency-graph/ready/next_batch, gate-exempt, excluded from Jira sync, hidden from default list' — `_FILE_IMPACT_EXEMPT_TYPES` is absent. This leaves a described guard site with no covering criterion.
findings.location: Section A ('add to `_FILE_IMPACT_EXEMPT_TYPES`'); AC1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concurrency guard (file lock, queue, or semaphore) around worktree git operations, OR cite and test the architectural invariant that guarantees single-threaded access — do not rely on an unvalidated serialization assumption.
findings.finding: The claim that 'the reviewbot's single background worker serializes reviews, so the shared worktree needs no extra lock' is stated as fact but is not validated by any probe, test fixture, or architectural reference. If the concurrency model changes (e.g., a second worker is added, or the background thread is replaced with async tasks), the shared persistent worktree becomes a corruption vector with no guard.
findings.location: Section D (Infra): 'Concurrency: the reviewbot's single background worker serializes reviews, so the shared worktree needs no extra lock.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `threading.Lock` (or `asyncio.Lock`) around the git add/commit/push sequence in the emit path, documented as the worktree serialization point. This is cheap insurance and makes the assumption explicit and enforceable regardless of future worker-count changes.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md section documenting the 'non-graph bulk artifact' ticket-type contract (the set of guard sites, the `_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES` constant, and the rationale) so future contributors can correctly add new artifact types.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel architectural decision — a new 'bulk artifact' ticket category (`code_review`) with its own store/graph/gate/Jira exemption pattern — but specifies no ADR, CLAUDE.md section, or design-doc update to capture this decision for future maintainers.
findings.location: Section A (The `code_review` ticket type) and AC item 1; no mention of ADR or doc update anywhere in the plan.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `docs/adr/NNN-bulk-artifact-ticket-type.md` (or a `## Bulk artifact ticket types` section in CLAUDE.md) listing: the guard sites, the `_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES` constant as the single source of truth, and the rationale (searchable/linkable in the same store, exempt from lifecycle/graph/Jira). Reference it from `reducer/_api.py` and `_engine_support/reads.py` in a comment.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ERROR-level log line (with a distinct marker/key, e.g., `code_review_emit_failure`) for each failed `create_ticket`, `link`, or `push` call, and document a log-based alert (or structured metric increment) so operators can detect systematic failures without log scraping.
findings.finding: The plan has no metric or alert for systematic artifact emit failures. Only WARNING/INFO log lines are specified; if `rebar.create_ticket` or `rebar.link` fails consistently (e.g., store API broken, credentials wrong), operators have no signal beyond log scraping.
findings.location: Section C (log format spec) and AC item 3; no metric/alert mentioned for create/link failures.
findings.suggested_fix: Emit `logger.error('code_review emit FAILED artifact_id=%s change=%s/%s error=%s', artifact_id, change_id, revision, exc)` on any non-retryable failure, using a consistent key (`code_review_emit_failure`) that a log-based alert can match on.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC3's test specification to cover: (a) `rebar.create_ticket` raises → exception is caught, vote succeeds, failure is logged (non-fatal guarantee); and (b) emitting for the same `(change-id, revision)` a second time reuses/finds the existing artifact and does not create a duplicate (idempotency guarantee).
findings.finding: AC3's unit-test specification covers only the happy path (resolvable ref → link) and the unresolved-ref warning path, but omits two behavioral guarantees stated in Section C: (1) artifact-creation failure (`rebar.create_ticket` raises) is caught and non-fatal — the vote must not be failed — and (2) the idempotency invariant — a re-review of the same `(change-id, revision)` must not create a duplicate artifact. Both are new user-facing behaviors with failure/invariant paths that have no specified test coverage.
findings.location: Section C ('Reviewbot emit + trailer linking') and AC3 ('Unit-tested with a fake store (resolvable ref → link; unresolved ref → logged, not raised)')
findings.suggested_fix: In `tests/unit/test_code_review_artifact.py`, add two test cases: `test_emit_create_failure_is_nonfatal` — fake store where `create_ticket` raises `StoreError`, assert the function returns without re-raising and that a warning was logged; and `test_emit_idempotent` — call emit twice with identical `(change_id, revision)`, assert `create_ticket` is called only once (the second call matches on title and reuses the existing artifact).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Instead of importing `norm_id` from `plan_review.sidecar` into `code_review.sidecar`, either duplicate the 4-line function locally in `code_review/sidecar.py` (the shared logic is stable and trivial) or extract it to a shared `llm/_fingerprint.py` utility — do not introduce a `code_review` → `plan_review` internals dependency for a single call-site.
findings.finding: `norm_id` from `plan_review/sidecar.py` is imported into `code_review/sidecar.py` as a cross-subpackage dependency, creating a 1-site coupling from `code_review` into `plan_review` internals for a 4-line function that could be co-located or placed in a shared utility.
findings.location: Section B — Join keys; `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py`
findings.suggested_fix: Copy the `norm_id` implementation (4 lines) directly into `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` as a local helper, or create `src/rebar/llm/_fingerprint.py` and import from there in both sidecars — whichever the team prefers — to avoid the cross-subpackage internal import.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add src/rebar/schemas/plan_review_verdict.schema.json and src/rebar/schemas/fetch_ticket_output.schema.json to the scope/files list (description string updates only) or explicitly call them out as known-acceptable drift.
findings.finding: Two schema description strings are not in the scope/files list but will contain stale type vocabulary after code_review is added: src/rebar/schemas/plan_review_verdict.schema.json (description: 'task | story | epic | bug | session_log.') and src/rebar/schemas/fetch_ticket_output.schema.json (description: 'The ticket type (task/story/bug/epic/session_log).'). These are not validated programmatically but create documentation drift.
findings.location: Scope/files list / src/rebar/schemas/plan_review_verdict.schema.json, src/rebar/schemas/fetch_ticket_output.schema.json

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either (a) rename the `include_session_logs` parameter to `include_artifact_logs` (with a deprecated alias) and state that rationale in the plan, or (b) add an explicit positive justification in the plan for why reusing `include_session_logs` to cover both artifact types is the correct tradeoff (e.g., 'avoids a breaking API change; both types are bulk artifacts that callers opt into together').
findings.finding: The `export_ndjson.py` guard (plan: 'broaden the hardcoded `session_log` guard so default NDJSON exports exclude `code_review` too (opt in via `include_session_logs`)') reuses the existing `include_session_logs` parameter to gate both artifact types. The parameter name is now semantically wrong (it gates code_review too), and callers that pass `include_session_logs=True` to get session logs will silently also receive all `code_review` artifacts. The plan states this change but provides no positive rationale for reusing the old parameter name versus adding a cleaner `include_artifact_logs` parameter (or renaming to `include_artifacts`).
findings.location: Section A — 'Export: broaden the hardcoded `session_log` guard in `_io/export_ndjson.py`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and document the git credential helper mechanism: confirm that `python:3.12-slim` ships no global `credential.helper`, add an explicit `git config --global --unset-all credential.helper || true` in the Dockerfile entrypoint before setting the URL-scoped helper, acknowledge that the helper script itself resides on disk (it is invoked as a subprocess), and confirm the token is not cached by any other git mechanism.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that a URL-scoped git credential helper (`credential.<url>.helper`) echoing `username=x-access-token` / `password=$REVIEWBOT_TICKETS_PAT` to stdout 'never lands on disk or in a remote URL', but this security claim is unverified. Git's credential system consults URL-scoped helpers preferentially but does not suppress a global `credential.helper` (e.g. `store`, `cache`, or `osxkeychain`) that may independently cache the token to disk after it is supplied. In a Debian/Ubuntu-based container (the Dockerfile uses `python:3.12-slim`), no global credential helper is configured by default — but the plan does not verify this assumption, nor does it specify that the entrypoint must explicitly unset any global helper before configuring the URL-scoped one. Additionally, the helper script itself must exist on disk as an executable file to be invoked by git, which the plan does not address.
findings.location: Section D (Infra) / Implementation precision — 'URL-SCOPED (origin only, NOT global) git credential helper that echoes username=x-access-token / password=$REVIEWBOT_TICKETS_PAT so the token never lands on disk or in a remote URL'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Dockerfile entrypoint (before the `git clone` that materializes the tickets worktree), add: `git config --global --unset-all credential.helper || true` followed by `git config --global credential.<tickets-url>.helper /usr/local/bin/rebar-tickets-cred-helper` where the helper script is a minimal `#!/bin/sh` that emits `username=x-access-token\npassword=$REVIEWBOT_TICKETS_PAT`. Explicitly note in the plan that the script resides on disk (acceptable — it contains no secret, only the env-var reference) and that the token itself is supplied at runtime via the env var, not hardcoded in the script.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit acceptance-criteria line and unit test asserting that `rebar.list_tickets(ticket_type="code_review")` returns existing `code_review` artifacts (i.e., that the `reads.py` visibility guard and the idempotency lookup are exercised together), to catch any partial-deploy scenario where the type is registered but the list guard is not yet broadened.
findings.finding: The plan's idempotency lookup for the `code_review` artifact (`rebar.list_tickets(ticket_type="code_review")` filtered by title) depends on `list_tickets` surfacing `code_review` tickets, but `list_tickets` currently passes `exclude_session_logs=(ticket_type != "session_log")` to `reduce_all_tickets`. After the plan's changes, this predicate must be broadened to `ticket_type not in _NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES` for `code_review` tickets to appear in the result. If the guard in `reads.py` is not updated simultaneously with the type's addition, `list_tickets(ticket_type="code_review")` returns an empty list and every review creates a duplicate artifact rather than reusing the existing one — silently degrading idempotency to a no-op with no error.
findings.location: Section C (Reviewbot emit) / Implementation precision — 'Idempotency lookup uses rebar.list_tickets(ticket_type="code_review")'; Section A — 'reads.py — hidden from default list, present in search/show'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a CloudWatch alarm (or extend the existing `voter_errors` host probe) to detect persistent `code_review` artifact-emit/push failures — e.g. a custom metric `rebar/host:artifact_emit_errors` published by the host observability probe grepping for the WARNING-level emit-failure log lines, with an alarm on non-zero count.
findings.finding: No CloudWatch alarm or metric is specified for persistent tickets-push failures (emit errors are logged non-fatally but never surfaced to the monitoring layer), meaning silent corpus degradation is undetectable by operators.
findings.location: Section D (Infra) — observability; Section C — 'All emit/link/push failures are caught and logged; they NEVER fail the review vote'; AC (no alarm specified for emit failures)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assert and document the post-rollback schema state: specify whether `code_review` tickets already written to the `tickets` branch must be manually archived/deleted on rollback, or add a compensating-forward note explaining that the store state is acceptable (visible in `list`/`export`) until the code is re-deployed — attach a literal `DATA LOSS RISK` note if purging is required.
findings.finding: The plan does not assert the schema/store state after a rollback once `code_review` tickets have been written to the `tickets` branch. Old code (pre-deploy) only filters `ticket_type == 'session_log'` in `reducer/_api.py` (line 244) and `reads.py` (line 373), so a rollback leaves `code_review` tickets visible in default `list` output, NDJSON exports, and graph/health hot-paths — a state the plan neither acknowledges nor mitigates.
findings.location: Section A — 'Store/graph exclusion: broaden the single reducer predicate at src/rebar/reducer/_api.py'; AC line 'excluded from the reducer/dependency-graph/ready/next_batch'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a rollback section to the plan: 'If rolled back after any `code_review` tickets are written, old code will surface them in `list` and `export`. Mitigation: (a) archive all `code_review` tickets via `rebar archive <id>` before rolling back, or (b) accept they are visible until re-deploy (no functional breakage — graph callers treat them as leaf-tier 0, same as unknown types). No DATA LOSS occurs; the records remain in the git history.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the required edit-ordering constraint (reads.py predicate broadening MUST land before voter.py artifact emit is active) and/or add a guard in the idempotency lookup (e.g., pass `exclude_session_logs=False` explicitly, or call the underlying reducer directly with the broadened predicate) so the lookup is not silently defeated by a partial deployment.
findings.finding: The idempotency lookup `rebar.list_tickets(ticket_type='code_review')` depends on `reads.py`'s `exclude_session_logs` predicate already being broadened; until that edit is applied, `code_review` tickets are excluded from the result (reads.py:373 evaluates `'code_review' != 'session_log'` → `True` → excluded), so the lookup always returns empty and the plan's 'fallback is to CREATE a fresh artifact' silently creates duplicates on every retry. The plan specifies no edit-ordering constraint or guard against this.
findings.location: Section C ('Idempotency: the artifact is keyed by (change-id, revision) — look up by the change-key title before creating'); Implementation precision section ('Idempotency lookup uses rebar.list_tickets(ticket_type="code_review")'); src/rebar/_engine_support/reads.py line 373
findings.suggested_fix: In the voter's idempotency lookup, call the store layer directly with an explicit `exclude_session_logs=False` (or use `reduce_all_tickets(..., exclude_session_logs=False)` filtered by `ticket_type=='code_review'`) rather than relying on `rebar.list_tickets` inheriting the broadened predicate — this makes the lookup robust to reads.py not yet being updated.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State whether `verify_commit.extract_ticket_refs` is an existing function (and provide its module path, e.g. `src/rebar/review_bot/verify_commit.py`) or must be created (and add it to the Scope/files list with the expected signature).
findings.finding: The plan calls `verify_commit.extract_ticket_refs(commit_message)` in Section C as if it is an existing function, but it is not listed in the Scope/files section and no module path is given. If this function does not already exist, an executor cannot proceed without discovering this at implementation time.
findings.location: Section C (Reviewbot emit + trailer linking) — 'verify_commit.extract_ticket_refs(commit_message)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical in Section C, e.g.: '`verify_commit.extract_ticket_refs(commit_message)` (existing: `src/rebar/review_bot/verify_commit.py`)' and include that path in the Scope/files emit block if it requires any edits.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add retry-with-backoff (e.g., 3 attempts, exponential) for the git push step, and specify worktree recovery on persistent failure (e.g., `git push --force-with-lease` or worktree reset to remote HEAD) so a single failed push does not block subsequent artifact emissions.
findings.finding: The plan specifies no retry or backoff for the git push step of artifact emission. A transient network failure during `git push` to the `tickets` branch will silently drop the artifact — the failure is caught and logged but not retried, and there is no recovery path (the local worktree commit exists but the push never succeeds).
findings.location: Section C: 'All emit/link/push failures are caught and logged; they NEVER fail the review vote.'
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the push in a retry loop (3 attempts, 1s/2s/4s backoff). On final failure, log an ERROR and attempt `git reset --hard origin/tickets` to restore the worktree to a pushable state, accepting the data loss for that review while keeping the worktree healthy for subsequent ones.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document an explicit rollback procedure (e.g. set `REBAR_EMIT_CODE_REVIEW_ARTIFACTS=false` and restart, or equivalent) so operators can disable artifact writes without a full code revert.
findings.finding: No rollback path is described for the artifact-emit feature: if the emit path causes operational issues post-deploy, the only recourse is a full container redeploy with a code revert. The plan explicitly excludes in-session verification of the live push but says nothing about how to quickly undo the behavior.
findings.location: Section D (Infra); AC bullet 4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES` meets Rule-of-Three (≥3 call-sites) or replace it with inline `not in ("session_log", "code_review")` at the two existing sites (`reducer/_api.py:244` and `reads.py:373`) to avoid premature abstraction.
findings.finding: `_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES` is a new shared cross-subpackage constant with only 2 call-sites (`reducer/_api.py` and `_engine_support/reads.py`), failing the Rule-of-Three threshold for justified abstraction.
findings.location: Section A — Store/graph exclusion; Implementation precision (`reducer/_api.py`, `reads.py`)
findings.suggested_fix: At `reducer/_api.py:244` change `r.get("ticket_type") != "session_log"` to `r.get("ticket_type") not in ("session_log", "code_review")`; at `reads.py:373` change the predicate to `ticket_type not in ("session_log", "code_review")`. Drop the shared constant unless a third artifact type lands in this same change.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Address how commit_message reaches the artifact-emit step on the merge-change path: either pass it through adapter.code_review_decision's merge-path call or thread it separately into the post-vote emit block, and add voter.py merge-path call-site to the scope.
findings.finding: The merge-change review path in voter.py does NOT pass commit_message to code_review_decision (confirmed at lines 430-437), so trailer extraction for merge changes would produce no refs. The plan's Section C describes trailer resolution for 'ALL commits in the change' but only the non-merge path currently receives commit_message. The voter emit step runs after both paths converge — if the merge-change path doesn't supply commit_message, the trailer union would be empty for merge changes.
findings.location: Section C / src/rebar/review_bot/voter.py lines 430-437

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
